text.title: Tailwind Core Patterns
text.summary: Rules and examples for cn() helper, cva variants, responsive design, dark mode, and @apply usage.

rule.statement: Always use cn() (twMerge + clsx) on every component that accepts a className prop.
rule.rationale: Without tailwind-merge, prop overrides silently lose when conflicting classes (p-4 p-2) are appended instead of replaced.

section.title: cva Variants

rule.statement: Use cva for any component with more than one variant axis; place base styles in the cva base array, not in individual variants.
rule.rationale: Inline ternaries multiply class strings and tank readability; cva keeps base/variant/size split explicit and diffable.

section.reference: Full Button component with cva, cn(), and TypeScript props

rule.statement: Use mobile-first breakpoint prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:) on every layout; never build desktop-only without responsive fallback.
rule.rationale: Mobile-first keeps the base class the smallest viewport; additions stack up, reducing override specificity issues.

example.code: {items.map(item => )}

rule.statement: Configure darkMode: 'class' in tailwind.config; always pair light-mode classes with dark: counterparts on every background and text color.
rule.rationale: Omitting dark: pairing is an invisible regression — elements look correct in light but broken in dark.

example.code: {children}

rule.statement: Limit @apply to semantic patterns in @layer components (e.g., .btn, .input, .card); never use it as a substitute for component extraction in TSX.
rule.rationale: @apply in JSX files defeats the purpose of utility-first composition and creates import-order-sensitive CSS specificity bugs.

section.title: Design Tokens

rule.statement: All colors and spacing must reference tokens in tailwind.config.ts; arbitrary values (w-[37px]) are forbidden without a code comment stating rationale.
rule.rationale: Arbitrary values escape the design system and are invisible to design-token sync pipelines (Figma → Style Dictionary → config).

section.reference: Tailwind config with brand tokens, dark mode, and plugins
